proposed_relations.description: ADA compliance statement for the 34th Street SBS project

evidence_refs.source_quote: the project will be fully compliant with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)

proposed_relations.description: DOT confidence claim that the 34th Street SBS plan will accommodate traffic demand

evidence_refs.source_quote: DOT is confident that the proposed plan, which maintains traffic in both directions from river to river, will accommodate traffic demand on 34 th Street without spillover effects onto parallel side streets.

decisions.rationale: Evidence quote references the proposed plan maintaining traffic on 34th Street, which is the SBS project design.

evidence_refs.source_quote: DOT is confident that the proposed plan, which maintains traffic in both directions from river to river, will accommodate traffic demand on 34 th Street without spillover effects onto parallel side streets.

proposed_relations.description: Claim about benefits of the curbside bus lane configuration on 34th Street

evidence_refs.source_quote: The current curbside bus lane configuration on 34 th Street provides significant benefits to bus riders, including decreased travel times, and has lead to growth in bus ridership on 34 th Street.

decisions.rationale: Claim describes the existing bus lane on 34th Street, providing context for the SBS project upgrades.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The current curbside bus lane configuration on 34 th Street provides significant benefits to bus riders, including decreased travel times, and has lead to growth in bus ridership on 34 th Street.

proposed_relations.description: Rule permitting private vehicles to use the bus lane for right turns and curb access on 34th Street

evidence_refs.source_quote: private vehicles will be permitted to use the bus lane when making the next available right turn and to access the curb

evidence_refs.source_quote: private vehicles will be permitted to use the bus lane when making the next available right turn and to access the curb

proposed_relations.description: Transfer policy for riders transferring from 34th Street SBS routes to M15 SBS

evidence_refs.source_quote: Riders making a transfer to the M15 SBS will have to obtain a separate receipt at a M15 SBS MetroCard fare collection machine or coin fare collection machine. Existing transfer policies, including free bus to bus transfer, will apply.

decisions.rationale: Claim describes fare and transfer procedures for the 34th Street SBS project, specifically for M16/M34 riders transferring to M15 SBS.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Riders making a transfer to the M15 SBS will have to obtain a separate receipt at a M15 SBS MetroCard fare collection machine or coin fare collection machine. Existing transfer policies, including free bus to bus transfer, will apply.

proposed_relations.description: Initial bus fleet description for 34th Street SBS service using M34 and M16 hybrid-electric buses

evidence_refs.source_quote: Initially service will operate with the hybrid-electric, low floor buses currently used on M34 and M16 routes. Should there be significant increases in ridership, low floor articulated buses would be used.

decisions.rationale: Claim describes the planned bus fleet for 34th Street SBS service, mentioning the M34 and M16 routes.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Initially service will operate with the hybrid-electric, low floor buses currently used on M34 and M16 routes. Should there be significant increases in ridership, low floor articulated buses would be used.

proposed_relations.description: Curb regulations for the 34th Street SBS project will prioritize loading over parking

evidence_refs.source_quote: DOT agrees. The curb regulations, which will be determined in consultation with the 34 th Street Community Advisory Committee, residents and other project stakeholders, will prioritize loading over parking.

decisions.rationale: Claim explicitly references the 34th Street Community Advisory Committee and project stakeholders, tying it to the SBS project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: DOT agrees. The curb regulations, which will be determined in consultation with the 34 th Street Community Advisory Committee, residents and other project stakeholders, will prioritize loading over parking.
